summary: NAQAL GO iteration 2 frontend retest - all 3 fixes from iteration_1 VERIFIED PASSING after Metro/Expo restart picked up new bundle. (1) Logout on web: tapping logout-btn now fires window.confirm with Arabic message 'Ù‡Ù„ Ø£Ù†Øª Ù…ØªØ£ÙƒØ¯ Ù…Ù† ØªØ³Ø¬ÙŠÙ„ Ø§Ù„Ø®Ø±ÙˆØ¬ØŸ'; accepting it calls signOut() and routes to /welcome (get-started-btn re-appears, URL = /welcome). (2) Map on web: /order/create now renders <iframe title='map' srcDoc=...> with leaflet-container inside (verified via frame_locator); the 'React Native WebView does not support this platform' banner is GONE (body innerText assertion). Both tile-loaded screenshot of Mosul map and 'Ø§Ù†Ù‚Ø± Ø¹Ù„Ù‰ Ø§Ù„Ø®Ø±ÙŠØ·Ø© Ù„ØªØ­Ø¯ÙŠØ¯ Ø§Ù„Ù…ÙˆÙ‚Ø¹' overlay visible. (3) Tab testIDs: bottom tabs now expose data-testid='tab-home', 'tab-orders', 'tab-profile' in DOM (Expo Router's tabBarButtonTestID propagates correctly on react-native-web). Regression happy-path Welcomeâ†’Phone(7700001234)â†’OTP(123456)â†’Homeâ†’service-furnitureâ†’mapâ†’backâ†’tab-profileâ†’logout-btnâ†’Welcome works end-to-end. Backend pytest suite not re-run this iteration (no backend changes) but remains at 24/24 from iteration_1.

issues: Console warnings persist (cosmetic, non-blocking): 'shadow*' style props deprecated â†’ use boxShadow; props.pointerEvents deprecated â†’ use style.pointerEvents; Image style.resizeMode deprecated â†’ use props.resizeMode.

action_items: (Optional/Cosmetic) Address the deprecated style-prop console warnings (shadow*, pointerEvents, Image.resizeMode) before app-store submission - non-blocking for Phase 1.

(Note for E1) Test agent had to `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` once before the new MapPicker/_layout bundle was served - Metro is running in CI mode with cache; if E1 ships further frontend changes please restart expo so testers see them.
critical_code_review_comments: MapPicker.tsx Platform.OS==='web' branch correctly returns <WebMap> (iframe srcDoc=...) using the same Leaflet HTML the native WebView uses - good DRY (buildHtml shared).
WebMap uses window.postMessage / addEventListener('message') with JSON.parse fallback - correct two-way bridge; cleanup in useEffect return is present (no listener leak).
(tabs)/_layout.tsx now uses options.tabBarButtonTestID per Tabs.Screen - this is the correct expo-router API and confirmed to emit data-testid on react-native-web.

profile.tsx logout: Platform.OS==='web' uses window.confirm (synchronous) before calling signOut/router.replace - works as expected on web; native still uses Alert.alert with destructive button. Both paths share doLogout().

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all 3 iteration_2 fixes + full happy-path regression

seed_data_creation: Reused user phone +9647700001234 (name 'Test User') from iteration_1.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1 MVP web-preview parity is fully achieved. For any future iteration: (a) login as 7700001234 / OTP 123456 - user already provisioned, no name screen will appear; (b) testIDs in use on home: home-user-name, quick-order-cta, service-furniture, service-goods, service-appliances, service-special, tab-home, tab-orders, tab-profile; on welcome: welcome-screen, get-started-btn; on phone: phone-input, send-otp-btn; on OTP: otp-input or otp-input-0..5, verify-otp-btn; on register: name-input, register-continue-btn/continue-btn; on profile: profile-user-name, logout-btn, profile-menu-{edit,addresses,...}; on order/create: back-btn, service-furniture map opens with iframe[title='map']. (c) Always restart expo with `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` after frontend code changes - Metro is in CI mode with caching. (d) Dialog handler `page.on('dialog', ...)` is required to auto-accept window.confirm logout dialog on web.
rca_of_issue: Initial run of the iteration_2 retest still showed the old broken behavior (no tab testIDs, WebView-unsupported banner, no iframe). Root cause: Metro is launched with CI=true (reloads disabled) and a local METRO_CACHE_ROOT=/app/frontend/.metro-cache, so changes to MapPicker.tsx and (tabs)/_layout.tsx were not hot-rebuilt. After `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` rebuilt the bundle, all three fixes verified green on the very next run with identical test script logic.
